title: Management Accounting Mock Mid Exam 2 – Advanced Costing Calculations
description: A calculation intensive quiz covering cost behavior, overhead absorption, activity based costing, job costing, and process costing.
subject: ACC2370 – Management Accounting

questions.question: A company incurs total costs of Rs 820000 at 40000 machine hours and Rs 940000 at 50000 machine hours. Using the high low method, what is the variable cost per machine hour?
questions.rationale: The change in cost divided by the change in activity gives the variable rate. Mixing fixed and variable components is a common mistake.

questions.question: Using the information in Question 1, what is the estimated fixed cost?
questions.rationale: Fixed cost is derived after removing the variable element from total cost at either activity level. Arithmetic slips often occur here.

questions.question: Total overheads of Rs 900000 are absorbed using machine hours. Budgeted machine hours are 45000. What is the overhead absorption rate per machine hour?
questions.rationale: Overhead rate equals total overhead divided by budgeted activity. Using actual hours instead of budgeted is a frequent error.

questions.question: If actual machine hours are 42000 and actual overhead incurred is Rs 880000, what is the amount of over or under absorption?
questions.rationale: Absorbed overhead is based on the rate multiplied by actual hours. Comparing absorbed and actual determines the variance.

questions.question: Direct material Rs 24000, direct labour Rs 16000, overhead is absorbed at 75 percent of direct labour cost. What is the prime cost?
questions.rationale: Prime cost includes only direct material and direct labour. Including overhead here is incorrect.

questions.question: Using the data in Question 5, what is the total production cost?
questions.rationale: Overhead must be added to prime cost to get total production cost. Percentage application errors are common.

questions.question: A job has direct material Rs 32000 issued, Rs 4000 returned, direct labour Rs 18000. Overhead is absorbed at 50 percent of prime cost. What is the job cost?
questions.rationale: Returned materials must be deducted before calculating prime cost. Overlooking returns overstates costs.

questions.question: Total setup cost is Rs 360000 and total production runs are 120. If Product X requires 15 runs, what setup cost is assigned to Product X?
questions.rationale: Setup rate per run multiplied by the number of runs gives assigned cost. Unit based thinking causes confusion.

questions.question: In an ABC system, ordering cost pool is Rs 300000 with 600 orders. If Product A requires 18 orders, what ordering cost is assigned?
questions.rationale: Cost per order multiplied by number of orders gives the assigned cost. Miscounting activity units is common.

questions.question: A batch of 500 units incurs fabrication cost of Rs 200000. What is the fabrication cost per unit?
questions.rationale: Dividing total cost by total units gives unit cost. Confusing batch cost with unit cost is a frequent trap.

questions.question: Opening work in progress is 300 units at 60 percent completion. Closing work in progress is 500 units at 40 percent completion. Units completed are 4200. What are equivalent units using weighted average method?
questions.rationale: Equivalent units include completed units plus equivalent of closing WIP. Ignoring completion percentages leads to errors.

questions.question: Total process cost is Rs 916000 and equivalent units are 4580. What is the cost per equivalent unit?
questions.rationale: Dividing total cost by equivalent units gives cost per unit. Rounding too early can mislead.

questions.question: Normal loss is 10 percent of input of 6000 units. Actual output is 5300 units. What is the abnormal loss or gain?
questions.rationale: Expected output is input minus normal loss. Comparing expected and actual output reveals abnormal loss or gain.

questions.question: Budgeted overhead is Rs 1200000 at 60000 labour hours. If actual labour hours are 55000, how much overhead is absorbed?
questions.rationale: Overhead absorption uses the predetermined rate multiplied by actual activity. Using actual overhead instead is incorrect.

questions.question: If direct labour hours are paid at Rs 120 per hour and 150 hours are worked on a job, what is the direct labour cost?
questions.rationale: Labour cost equals hours multiplied by rate. Simple arithmetic errors are common under pressure.

questions.question: Total overhead Rs 750000 is absorbed using [dropdown_1]. If budgeted activity is 30000 hours, the overhead rate is [dropdown_2] per hour.
questions.rationale: The rate is derived by dividing overhead by the chosen activity base.

questions.question: A job has prime cost Rs 50000 and overhead absorbed at [dropdown_1] percent of prime cost. Total job cost is Rs 72500. The overhead absorbed is [dropdown_2].
questions.rationale: Overhead is calculated as a percentage of prime cost and added to derive total cost.

questions.question: Equivalent units are calculated to value [dropdown_1] and [dropdown_2] in process costing.
questions.rationale: Equivalent units convert incomplete production into comparable completed units.

questions.question: If overhead absorbed exceeds actual overhead incurred, the result is ________ absorption.
questions.rationale: When absorbed overhead is higher than actual, it is termed over absorption.

questions.question: The formula for overhead absorption rate is total overhead divided by ________.
questions.rationale: Predetermined rates rely on budgeted activity to ensure consistency.

questions.question: Normal loss units are valued at ________ cost in process costing.
questions.rationale: Normal loss has no cost assigned and is absorbed by good output.

questions.question: Which of the following figures are required to calculate an overhead absorption rate?
questions.rationale: Predetermined rates rely on budgeted figures, not actual results.

questions.question: Which elements are included when calculating prime cost?
questions.rationale: Prime cost excludes all overheads and indirect costs.

questions.question: Which of the following will increase total variable cost?
options: Increase in variable cost per unit
answer: Increase in variable cost per unit
questions.rationale: Variable cost changes with activity and rate. Fixed costs do not affect variable total.

questions.question: A department absorbs overhead at Rs 18 per labour hour. If 4200 labour hours are worked, how much overhead is absorbed?
questions.rationale: Multiplying the rate by actual hours gives absorbed overhead. Simple multiplication errors are common.
